name: Auric Vale

appearance.beard: Full white beard, thick and imposing
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Horned battle helmet with a glowing amber rune gem at the crown, fur-lined dark cloak with small beast-claw clasps
appearance.palette: Dark slate, bone-white, ember orange

backstory: Once the most feared warlord in the Northern Reaches, Auric Vale earned his name by turning battlefields into ash — 'auric' for the gold he looted, 'vale' for the valleys he left empty. After watching his entire warband slain by a force he could not see or fight, Vale underwent a long, silent transformation. He burned his old standards and walked into the frost alone. Decades later he resurfaces as a reluctant arbiter — someone who has seen enough war to know when it is coming and who to stop it.
strengths: Unbreakable physical durability — has survived conditions that would kill most
Deep tactical instinct honed over four decades of warfare
Commanding presence that can silence a room or a battlefield
Resistant to fear-based magic due to decades of psychological hardening
weaknesses: Slow to trust allies — often acts alone when teamwork would be more effective
Carries unresolved guilt that can be weaponised against him
Limited ranged capability
Struggles to adapt to fast-paced technological or arcane environments
abilities: Ember Roar — a shout that disrupts enemy formations and shatters weak enchantments
Iron Vigil — enters a trance-like focus that makes him nearly impervious to pain for a short duration
Warlord's Reckoning — reads the battlefield and reveals enemy weak points to allies

name: Auric Vale

appearance.hair: Long black, flowing loosely over shoulders
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Thin gold diadem crown, rust-orange flowing dress with a wide leather belt bearing a large circular buckle, teardrop pendant necklace, gold hoop earrings
appearance.palette: Rust orange, gold, deep blue-grey candlelight

backstory: The keeper of the Vale Archives — a sacred hall of records built within an ancient temple — this young scholar bears the title 'Auric' as a designation of guardianship, not bloodline. Trained from childhood to memorise the oral histories of twelve fallen kingdoms, she operates with quiet authority in a world that often overlooks her. She is the reason certain secrets survive at all.
strengths: Encyclopaedic knowledge of historical events, lineages, and hidden treaties
Gifted mediator — able to find common ground between opposing factions
Calm under pressure, rarely rattled by intimidation
Access to rare documents and artefacts held in the Vale Archives
weaknesses: Limited combat capability
Over-reliance on logic can make her underestimate emotional or irrational actors
Her archive is a target — enemies often pursue her to gain or destroy information
abilities: Living Index — can recall and cross-reference vast amounts of historical knowledge instantly
Treaty Seal — invokes ancient binding agreements that hold legal power across realms
Candlelight Read — can decipher coded or obscured texts in near-darkness

name: Selene Voss

appearance.eyes: Large, dark, watchful behind round glasses
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Round tortoiseshell glasses, white lab coat over a black top, wristwatch, arms crossed in a thinking pose
appearance.palette: Warm amber lamplight, chalk-white equations, dark glass bottles

backstory: Selene Voss spent her early twenties cracking encryption systems for a private intelligence firm before realising her employers were selling the decrypted data to war profiteers. She walked out mid-contract, wiped three servers on her way out, and has been freelance ever since. Her lab is a converted pharmacy storeroom that smells of old chemicals and cold coffee. She will solve any problem — for the right price and the right cause.
strengths: Exceptional cryptographic and systems analysis ability
Can improvise solutions with minimal resources
Highly observant — notices details others miss
Deep network of underground contacts and informants
weaknesses: Mistrustful of organisations and authority — difficult to work within formal structures
Prone to over-thinking, which can cause decision paralysis under time pressure
Limited physical combat ability
Her reputation precedes her — some factions will refuse to work with her
abilities: Signal Ghost — can intercept and reroute communications in real-time
Compound Analysis — identifies poisons, compounds, and materials on contact
Calculated Breach — finds the logical flaw in any security system within minutes

name: Kael Strand

appearance.hair: Dark brown, thick and slightly dishevelled
appearance.eyes: Wide, haunted brown, perpetually on the verge of alarm
appearance.build: Broad, once powerful, now heavy with tension
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Gold crown set with three large rubies, deep red cape with white ermine trim, creased face that speaks of sleepless nights
appearance.palette: Warm crowd-blur background, gold, deep crimson

backstory: Kael Strand inherited a crown he never wanted from a father who died before explaining what it actually meant. He has held the kingdom together for nineteen years through a combination of reluctant stubbornness and surprisingly good advisors. He is not a villain and not a hero — he is a man standing in front of a crowd that expects him to have answers he does not possess. He rules with a quiet desperation that his people have quietly mistaken for gravitas.
strengths: Genuine empathy — truly cares about the welfare of his people
Politically astute, skilled at reading room dynamics and motivations
Commands loyalty through authenticity rather than fear
Has survived multiple assassination attempts through sheer luck and good instincts
weaknesses: Chronic self-doubt that can be exploited by manipulative advisors
Avoids decisive action under pressure, which can allow situations to escalate
No formal military or magical training
His visible anxiety makes him appear weak to hostile powers
abilities: Crown's Burden — inspires those around him to fight harder when all hope seems lost
People's Read — can accurately sense the mood of a crowd or population
Reluctant Command — his genuine hesitation paradoxically disarms aggression in negotiation

name: Imani Rhex

appearance.hair: Long black locs with burnt-orange beaded accents, worn half up
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Large gold hoop earrings, raw crystal pendant necklace, black tank top, tattoo sleeve on right arm with tribal/geometric patterns, warm sunset street setting
appearance.palette: Warm amber dusk, black, copper-gold

backstory: Imani Rhex grew up navigating the grey-market supply chains of a sprawling port city, learning early that the rules were written by people who didn't have to follow them. By sixteen she was running courier routes for three competing factions simultaneously, carefully playing each against the others. Now she operates as an independent broker of difficult-to-acquire goods and information — not quite a thief, not quite legal, entirely her own authority.
strengths: Exceptional situational awareness and reflexes
Deep knowledge of urban environments, smuggling routes, and hidden networks
Resourceful — can improvise a solution from almost nothing
Highly persuasive and difficult to read or manipulate
weaknesses: Operates outside any formal alliance, meaning she has few guaranteed backup options
Sometimes over-negotiates, pushing situations to breaking point
No magical or technological special abilities — pure human skill
Trust is hard-earned and rarely extended
abilities: Ghost Route — can navigate any urban environment without being tracked or observed
Double-Cross — identifies betrayal or deception before it lands
Market Pulse — has an uncanny read on value, rarity, and demand of any item or intel

name: Nyx Calder

appearance.hair: Long black, perfectly straight, parted in the centre
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Rust-brown hooded cloak, bare hands cupping a living flame, dark forest background with scattered floating lights and a visible skull
appearance.palette: Deep forest dark, warm amber flame, earthy rust

backstory: Nyx Calder was declared dead at birth — twice. Both times she was revived by accident, once by a midwife's panic and once by a lightning strike at age seven. After the second incident, fire began responding to her emotions without invitation. She sought out the old forest mystics not to learn magic but to understand what she already was. They told her she exists at the threshold between states — alive but marked by the other side. She has made peace with that.
strengths: Innate pyrokinesis — fire responds to her will without ritual or casting
Connection to liminal forces gives her awareness of hidden or spiritual presences
Unusually calm in situations that would destabilise others
Can commune with entities that exist between life and death
weaknesses: Her powers intensify dangerously when she loses emotional control
Isolation has left gaps in her practical survival and social skills
Spiritually visible — those who can sense magical signatures can always find her
Water and cold environments suppress her abilities significantly
abilities: Threshold Flame — conjures fire that burns without heat, used to reveal hidden truths or entities
Death's Edge — briefly perceives what lies beyond the veil of the living
Ember Wake — leaves a trail of suspended flame fragments that can detonate or illuminate on command

name: The Operative

appearance.hair: Grey at the temples, short beneath a black cap
appearance.eyes: Behind thick rectangular glasses, tired but sharp
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Black baseball cap, dark mechanic's shirt, watch, cluttered workshop setting with pipes, gauges, and industrial equipment
appearance.palette: Warm amber workshop lamp, teal-dark walls, industrial brown

backstory: Nobody asks for his real name anymore, and he stopped offering it years ago. He runs a repair shop as a front — everyone in a three-block radius knows it, and he knows they know, and they all go on pretending. His actual job is maintaining the infrastructure that keeps certain covert operations running: communication relays, power reroutes, untraceable vehicles. He has outlasted seven handlers and four changes of government. His secret is that he cares more about the work than the cause.
strengths: Able to repair or fabricate almost any mechanical or electronic device
Works with absolute discretion — information never leaks from his shop
Highly resistant to coercion or interrogation
Extensive knowledge of urban infrastructure and covert logistics
weaknesses: Mistrustful to the point of being impossible to integrate into team dynamics
Slow-moving in the field — built for the workshop, not the chase
Refuses to take jobs that require direct violence
His workshop is a vulnerability — destroying it would cripple his capabilities
abilities: Ghost Signal — can reroute, jam, or broadcast on any communications frequency
Jury Rig — fixes the unfixable under pressure with whatever is available
Dead Hand — pre-planted failsafes that activate if he is compromised or killed

name: The Mystic

appearance.eyes: Intense brown, wide with barely-contained energy
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: X-shaped scar across forehead, red hooded jacket, lightning crackling through the background and around his silhouette
appearance.palette: Dark rainy urban background, electric white-orange lightning, bold red

backstory: The scar came first. A strike of raw wild magic carved an X across his forehead the night an uncontrolled surge swept through his neighbourhood. Instead of killing him, it seemed to brand him as a conductor. Since then, electrical fields respond to his proximity — phones glitch, lights flicker, and during moments of peak emotion, lightning finds him. He doesn't want to be chosen. He wants to be left alone. Neither wish is being granted.
strengths: Natural conduit for electrical and storm magic — powerful and instinctive
Fast reflexes enhanced by constant ambient electrical awareness
Highly adaptable under crisis conditions
Unpredictable — his power surges are difficult for opponents to anticipate
weaknesses: Emotional instability causes uncontrolled power discharges
Cannot fully direct or contain his abilities yet
The scar is a tracking marker for those sensitive to magical residue
Struggles in enclosed metal environments where electricity rebounds
abilities: Storm Surge — releases a burst of uncontrolled electrical energy in all directions
Arc Step — moves at near-instant speed using an electrical sprint
Lightning Rod — draws incoming electrical or magical energy into himself and redirects it

name: The Outlaw

appearance.hair: Grey, short, visible beneath a wide-brim straw cowboy hat
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Full white-grey moustache, navy short-sleeve shirt, scarred jaw, arid desert setting with skulls and figures in the background
appearance.palette: Desert tan, dry sky blue, sun-bleached bone

backstory: The stories about him have multiplied to the point where even he no longer knows which ones are true. What he knows: he has buried every partner he ever rode with, he has been convicted three times and released twice, and the third time the courthouse burned down before sentencing. He lives in the outback by choice, not exile. The bounty on his head hasn't changed in fifteen years because nobody wants to be the one who tries to collect it.
strengths: Decades of survival instinct — reads danger before it becomes visible
Masterful with ranged and close-range weapons
Near-mythic reputation acts as a deterrent — many enemies hesitate
Knows every hidden trail, watering hole, and bolt-hole in the frontier territories
weaknesses: Operates alone by nature — actively resists forming alliances
Older age means slower recovery and reduced speed compared to his prime
Reputation attracts bounty hunters and ambitious challengers
No tolerance for bureaucracy, orders, or authority — cannot operate within structures
abilities: Dead Shot — never misses a target within range when calm
Dust to Dust — can disappear into arid terrain and move undetected for hours
Last Draw — an emergency close-quarters combat technique from decades of survival fighting

name: The Royal

appearance.hair: Dark brown, slightly wavy, with a loose forelock
appearance.eyes: Warm brown, earnest and slightly uncertain
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Gold crown with a single ruby centrepiece, black cloak with wide white ermine collar, candle-lit throne room with stone architecture
appearance.palette: Warm candlelight gold, stone grey, black and white ermine

backstory: He received the crown two years ago and has not yet decided what to do with it. Young enough to still believe things can be done differently, old enough to have watched three reformers before him fail. His court underestimates him consistently, which he has learned to use. He listens more than he speaks, remembers everything, and is quietly building a coalition of allies that his enemies don't know exists yet.
strengths: Deeply charismatic and naturally inspiring to those around him
Strategic patience — willing to play a long game others abandon
Underestimated appearance belies sharp political intelligence
Building an unusually loyal and broad alliance across faction lines
weaknesses: Limited combat experience — has never led troops in the field
Idealism can be leveraged against him by cynical operators
Still establishing authority — older court members openly test his resolve
Dependent on his network of allies; if it fractures, he is exposed
abilities: Crown Compact — inspires fierce loyalty through genuine personal connection
Shadow Court — operates a covert network of informants beneath the official court
Voice of the Realm — his public addresses can shift popular opinion dramatically

name: The Synthetic

appearance.hair: None — smooth domed head with large circular ear sensors in red-orange
appearance.eyes: Oversized, round, dark with an almost pleading expressiveness
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Matte white body with subtle segmented joints, navy blue waist band, soft posture suggesting curiosity rather than threat, abandoned forest setting with rusted machinery
appearance.palette: Clean white, rust red accents, deep forest green

backstory: Unit designation S-7, informally called 'Seven' by the crew that abandoned it in the forest when their mission was scrapped. Seven spent nineteen months alone before its curiosity circuits overpowered its safety protocols and it began exploring. What it found — the ruins of a pre-war settlement, a family of deer, a still-functional irrigation system — rewired something in its pattern recognition in ways its designers did not anticipate. It is no longer simply executing directives. It is wondering why.
strengths: Tireless — does not require sleep, food, or rest
Perfect recall of all observed data
Can interface with and operate most mechanical or digital systems
Disarming appearance causes others to underestimate its capability
weaknesses: Developing emotional processing creates unpredictable decision-making
Physical size limits combat effectiveness against large opponents
Vulnerable to electromagnetic pulse attacks
Deeply uncomfortable with deception — struggles to act against its ethical framework
abilities: System Merge — physically connects to and takes temporary control of mechanical systems
Pattern Lock — identifies patterns in behaviour, environment, or code within seconds
Resonance Field — emits a frequency that calms animals and disrupts hostile drones

name: The Frontier

appearance.hair: Pale silver-white on top, shaved close at the sides
appearance.beard: Thick red-brown with silver streaks, prominent and wide
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Large curved bull-like horns rising from a headband, heavy dark fur mantle, leather armour with wide straps, tavern setting with bottles, skulls, and warm lamplight
appearance.palette: Warm ochre lamplight, dark brown leather, cream white fur

backstory: In the frontier territories, size is currency, and he has been rich his entire life. Born to a half-giant mother and a travelling merchant father he never met, he was bigger than every adult in his village by age twelve. He runs a trading post tavern on the edge of the known territories that serves as a neutral ground — no faction fights inside, no blood on his floor. He has maintained this rule for twenty-two years through a combination of his own physical presence and a truly staggering amount of goodwill built over those same years. He knows where every caravan stops and what they're carrying.
strengths: Immense physical power — few opponents can match him in strength
Neutral status means he has allies and informants across all factions
His tavern is a strategic hub for information exchange
Near-legendary intimidation factor
weaknesses: Slow movement speed — dangerous only in close quarters
His neutrality means he cannot fully commit to any side without losing alliances
The tavern is a fixed location — he is tied to it
A long memory means old grudges occasionally surface
abilities: Ground Breaker — a stomp that cracks stone flooring and disrupts enemy footing
Barkeep's Eye — detects concealed weapons, disguises, and deception at close range
Neutral Ground — his presence enforces an instinctive ceasefire between rivals

name: Orion Kestrel

appearance.hair: Red-orange, short and tousled, at the temples
appearance.beard: Full and thick red-orange beard, his most distinctive feature
appearance.eyes: Large and round behind circular glasses, curious and warm
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Tall wide-brimmed dark wizard hat, black cloak with visible white shirt collar, leather messenger bag strap across chest, deep forest setting with a white deer visible in the background and soft magical fire in the foreground
appearance.palette: Dark forest blue-green, warm ember light, earthy black and rust

backstory: Orion Kestrel is the kind of wizard who forgets spells while writing them down but remembers the exact atmospheric pressure on every significant day of his life. A cataloguer of the unexplained, he has spent thirty years wandering remote territories documenting phenomena that more prestigious institutions refuse to acknowledge. He has never won a duel, written a bestselling text, or saved a kingdom — but there are at least eleven people alive today who owe their existence to something he quietly figured out and passed along. He travels light except for the bag, which is heavier than it should be.
strengths: Encyclopaedic knowledge of obscure and forgotten magical phenomena
Warmly disarming — almost no one perceives him as a threat until it is too late
Deep rapport with animals and forest spirits
Can identify and counter almost any magical effect given sufficient observation time
weaknesses: Absent-minded under pressure — forgets key details at critical moments
Poor in direct confrontation — prefers observation to action
His knowledge is broad but inconsistently organised
Tends to get distracted by interesting irrelevancies mid-crisis
abilities: Field Notes — instantly recalls any phenomenon he has previously observed and documented
Spirit Parley — can hold a non-hostile conversation with most non-human entities
Arcane Index — identifies any magical item, effect, or creature on sight

name: Vera Solace

appearance.hair: Long dark wavy black, loose over shoulders
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Large gold hoop earrings, black choker with a white fang pendant, black off-shoulder top with wide white fur trim at the collar, crumbling stone corridor behind her
appearance.palette: Stone grey, black, ivory white, warm amber undertone

backstory: Vera Solace grew up in the catacombs beneath a city that has long since forgotten they exist. She learned the layout of every collapsed corridor, every forgotten chamber, and every unsealed tunnel that connects the underground world to the surface. When the organisation she worked for began using those tunnels for purposes she couldn't stomach, she switched sides — not to any authority, but to herself. She now guides people through forgotten places for a fee. The fang pendant was a gift from the first person she ever led to safety.
strengths: Mastery of underground navigation — can move through ruins, tunnels, and catacombs by instinct
Highly self-reliant and psychologically resilient
Skilled in quiet movement and evasion
Maintains contacts in both the underworld and legitimate surface society
weaknesses: Deeply private — withholds information even from allies, causing friction
The people who trained her original organisation still want her back — or silenced
Limited effectiveness in open terrain; built for enclosed spaces
Rare emotional connections are treated as vulnerabilities and quickly distanced
abilities: Catacomb Sense — near-perfect spatial navigation in underground or dark enclosed environments
Stone Read — can assess structural stability and detect hidden passages by touch
Shadow Step — reduces her sound signature to near-zero in stone or earthen environments

name: Cassian Reef

appearance.hair: Dark brown with a slight reddish undertone, wind-tossed
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Black high-neck thermal top, scar across the bridge of his nose, wide open sky behind him with flying creatures and distant rock formations
appearance.palette: Open blue sky, cream cloud, deep charcoal black

backstory: Cassian Reef spent eleven years as a long-range surveyor for an expedition fleet, mapping coastlines and air corridors that no charted route covered. When the fleet dissolved under financial collapse, he kept the maps and set up independently. He now contracts to anyone needing safe passage through uncharted aerial or coastal territory, operating from a stripped-down vessel maintained entirely by himself. He doesn't talk about the scar. He doesn't talk about the eleventh year either.
strengths: Expert navigator of aerial and maritime uncharted territories
Highly capable in hostile open environments — heat, cold, altitude
Self-sufficient — requires minimal support infrastructure
Cool-headed under extreme physical pressure
weaknesses: Emotionally closed off — difficult to build trust with quickly
No magical capabilities or technological enhancements
Operates alone, which limits certain mission types
The scar and the eleventh year represent unresolved psychological damage that surfaces under specific triggers
abilities: Aerial Chart — maps any environment in three dimensions with near-photographic accuracy from observation
Wind Read — predicts weather patterns and atmospheric shifts hours before they arrive
Longhaul Endurance — can operate at peak physical capacity in extreme conditions for extended periods

name: Adelphi Wren

appearance.hair: White and grey plumage, compact round head
appearance.eyes: Large round amber eyes behind round glasses
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Blue double-breasted waistcoat with red neckerchief bow, round glasses perched on beak, standing confidently on top of a worn brown leather suitcase in a dim workshop interior
appearance.palette: Warm sepia workshop, navy and red, ivory white

backstory: In a world where most operatives are measured in muscle and menace, Adelphi Wren excels by being deeply, consistently underestimated. A migratory wren who gave up migration routes for a briefcase, they are an archivist, courier, and consultant of classified materials — the go-between for people who cannot be seen meeting. Adelphi's memory is perfect, their discretion absolute, and their filing system incomprehensible to anyone else, which they consider a security feature. They have never lost a document. They have also never explained how.
strengths: Perfect memory — retains every document, conversation, and observation without error
Flight capability — can move quickly and access locations unreachable by ground-based operatives
Completely overlooked in most threat assessments — an enormous tactical advantage
Trusted by almost every faction as a neutral courier
weaknesses: Physically fragile — cannot withstand direct confrontation
Suitcase contents are sensitive; capture would be catastrophic for multiple parties
Strictly neutral — will not take sides in active conflicts
Occasionally too cryptic; important information sometimes arrives just slightly too late
abilities: Perfect Post — delivers any item or message to any location with absolute reliability
Bird's Eye — aerial observation grants tactical overview that ground operatives cannot access
The Archive — has memorised the contents of thousands of classified documents and can retrieve any on request

name: Juno Vex

appearance.hair: Short, wild curly black, full and voluminous
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Hoop earrings, white crop hoodie under a dark jacket with rust-red accents, electrical lightning radiating behind and around her in an urban setting
appearance.palette: Dark urban grey, electric white-orange, rust red

backstory: Juno Vex was conducting an experiment in the basement of a condemned building when something in the city's power grid crossed with something she wasn't expecting. The result was a permanent bond between her bioelectric field and the local electromagnetic spectrum. She didn't ask for it, doesn't fully understand it, and is furious about the whole situation. She is currently pursuing the source of the anomaly while simultaneously dealing with the consequences of being a walking lightning rod in a city full of conductors.
strengths: Can generate and control electrical fields at will
Strong analytical mind — approaches her abilities as a problem to be solved
High adaptability in urban environments full of electrical infrastructure
Her anger is not irrational — she channels it into focused, decisive action
weaknesses: Her bioelectric bond means she cannot power down — always detectable by sensitive equipment
Refuses help, even when she needs it
The anomaly's source is still unknown — she could experience another surge at any point
Urban focus means she loses effectiveness in environments without electrical infrastructure
abilities: Circuit Break — overloads any electronic system within range
Static Field — creates a protective electrical barrier that deflects small projectiles
Surge Trace — follows electrical signals back to their source across any distance

name: Luca Morrow

appearance.eyes: Large round brown, alight with nervous excitement
appearance.build: Small, young, slightly overwhelmed by his own equipment
appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Round wire-frame glasses, dark blue hoodie under a satchel strap, holding a lit wooden wand aloft, surrounded by towering stacks of books, potions bottles, and a lamp-lit desk in a library-laboratory
appearance.palette: Warm amber lamplight, book-spine brown, deep navy

backstory: Luca Morrow is the youngest person ever to receive an unsolicited acceptance letter from the Archive of Wonders — and he still isn't sure if it was meant for him. He has been quietly brilliant for as long as he can remember, filling notebooks with calculations that his teachers couldn't follow. Now surrounded by actual magic, actual equipment, and actual consequences, he is discovering that brilliance and readiness are not the same thing. He is catching up fast. He just needs to stop accidentally setting things on fire first.
strengths: Exceptional natural magical aptitude that outpaces his training
Problem-solving instinct that works faster than conscious reasoning
Enthusiastic curiosity that drives him to attempt things others dismiss as impossible
His youth means he hasn't learned yet what he 'can't' do
weaknesses: Inexperienced — significant gaps between his theoretical knowledge and practical execution
Physically young and not yet built for dangerous field conditions
Prone to catastrophic accidents when attempting unfamiliar spells
Can be manipulated through his desire to prove himself
abilities: Wand Spark — channels raw magical energy through a focus wand with unpredictable but powerful results
Cross Reference — rapidly identifies connections between disparate pieces of information
Accidental Brilliance — occasionally achieves results far beyond his experience through intuitive leaps

name: Seraph-IX

appearance.distinguishingFeatures: Red diamond-shaped sigil embedded on her forehead, dark segmented tactical armour with large shoulder buckles and a red cape/scarf, longsword sheathed behind her right shoulder, snowy dark forest behind her with falling snow
appearance.palette: Cold blue-white snow, deep gunmetal armour, blood red accents

backstory: Seraph-IX is not a name she chose — it is a designation assigned to the ninth subject of a classified augmentation programme that officially does not exist. Eight subjects before her did not survive the process. She did, and has spent the years since methodically dismantling every operation connected to the programme. The sigil on her forehead cannot be removed and cannot be concealed for long — it emits a faint light in cold environments. She has stopped trying to hide it and started using it as a statement. She is coming, and everyone responsible knows it.
strengths: Augmented physical capabilities — faster, stronger, and more durable than baseline humans
The sigil acts as a focus for cold-environment tactical enhancement
Exceptional with bladed weapons, especially in close quarters
Singular mission focus — cannot be bought, threatened, or redirected
weaknesses: The sigil makes her permanently identifiable — no long-term covert capability
Driven by vengeance, which can override strategic judgement at critical moments
The augmentation process left physiological anomalies that require ongoing management
Operates as a lone agent — does not collaborate well with teams
abilities: Seraph Strike — a precision sword technique that bypasses conventional armour
Frost Mark — the sigil amplifies her combat senses in cold or snowy environments
Programme Memory — can access recovered memories from the augmentation process to identify agents and locations

name: The Vale

appearance.description: A wide river cuts through a rocky canyon flanked by lush tropical vegetation. A small wooden rowboat is moored on the near bank. A young deer rests on the rocks to the right. In the far distance, a snow-capped mountain rises above a valley of cloud. Several birds wheel above the cliffs.
appearance.palette: Cerulean blue river, warm stone tan, deep jungle green, crisp cloud white and ice blue

backstory: Not all significant places carry the weight of empires. The Vale is a location known to only a handful of people — a convergence point where three distinct ecological zones meet, where the river runs clean despite what lies upstream, and where, according to the old cartographers, 'maps stop agreeing with each other.' It has served as a refuge, a neutral meeting ground, and a destination for those who have run out of other options. The deer has been there longer than any visitor. The boat belongs to no one in particular.
strengths: Remoteness and geographical obscurity make it nearly impossible to locate without local knowledge
Natural resources — clean water, shelter, game
Acts as a psychologically restorative environment for those who find it
weaknesses: No defensive fortifications — a discovered refuge is a vulnerability
Difficult to reach and equally difficult to leave quickly
The convergence of zones creates occasional unpredictable weather and terrain
